content.problemStatement:
Chef has a list of integers and wants to find the maximum XOR value that can be obtained by XORing any two integers from this list. 

Chef is curious to know the highest possible XOR result between any pair of elements, including the possibility of XORing an element with itself. Help Chef determine this maximum XOR value for multiple test cases.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMaxXorValuefindMaxXorValuefindMaxXorValue - This function calculates the maximum XOR value obtainable by XORing any two integers in the given list.

Parameters
numbersnumbersnumbers : A reference to a vector containing integers.
Represents the list of integers to evaluate.
Each integer can range within typical 32-bit signed integer limits.
Return Value
Returns a number representing the maximum XOR value found between any two elements in numbersnumbersnumbers.
The output is a single integer value indicating the highest XOR result possible.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105 where TTT is the number of test cases.
1≤N≤2×1051 \leq N \leq 2 \times 10^51≤N≤2×105 where NNN is the size of the integer list for each test case.
0≤numbers[i]≤231−10 \leq \text{numbers}[i] \leq 2^{31} - 10≤numbers[i]≤231−1 for each element in the integer list.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2×1052 \times 10^52×105.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer NNN, the size of Chef's integer list.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing Chef's integer list.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum XOR value that Chef can obtain by XORing any two integers from the given list.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, the maximum XOR is 7, obtained by XORing 3 and 4 (3 ^ 4 = 7).
For the second test case, the maximum XOR is 15, obtained by XORing 8 and 7 (8 ^ 7 = 15).

Explanation:
For the array [1023, 512, 256, 128, 64, 32, 16, 8], the maximum XOR is found by XORing 1023 and 0 (or any smaller number that differs in all bits), resulting in 1023.
No other pair produces a higher XOR value than 1023 here.
